steps.title: Find the y-intercept b from the graph
steps.content: The y-intercept is the value of $y$ when $x = 0$. Looking at the graph, the line crosses the y-axis at the point $(0, -3)$, so $b = -3$.

steps.title: Find the slope a using two points
steps.content:
To find slope, identify a second point on the line. The line passes through $(1, 3)$. Using the slope formula:
$$a = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1} = \frac{3 - (-3)}{1 - 0} = \frac{6}{1} = 6$$

steps.title: Calculate a − b
steps.content:
Now compute the difference:
$$a - b = 6 - (-3) = 6 + 3 = 9$$
The answer is 9.
